# Apple Inc. — Form 10-Q Excerpt (synthetic sample)

Filing date: 2026-02-15
Period ended: 2025-12-27
Source: synthetic sample created for the NovaVest Research prototype. Not a real SEC filing — the figures below are illustrative. Used to exercise the REVISE path (one important risk category missing).

## Results of operations
Total revenue for the quarter was $124.3 billion, up 4% year over year. iPhone revenue declined 1%, while Services revenue grew 11%. Gross margin was 46.9%, an expansion of 50 basis points driven by Services mix. Operating margin was 31.0%.

## Liquidity and capital resources
Cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities totaled $172.8 billion at quarter end. The company returned $27.0 billion to shareholders through dividends and buybacks during the quarter. Long-term debt stood at $98.4 billion.

## Risk factors
The company is a defendant in patent litigation relating to wireless communications technology. Plaintiffs in the matter are seeking unspecified damages. The litigation is in early stages and the range of loss is not yet determinable.

## Forward guidance
Management expects total revenue growth in the low-single-digit range for the next quarter. Services revenue is forecast to continue double-digit growth.

Total revenue for the quarter was $124.3 billion, up 4% year over year. iPhone revenue declined 1%, while Services revenue grew 11%. Gross margin was 46.9%, an expansion of 50 basis points driven by Services mix. Operating margin was 31.0%.

Cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities totaled $172.8 billion at quarter end. The company returned $27.0 billion to shareholders through dividends and buybacks during the quarter. Long-term debt stood at $98.4 billion.

The company is a defendant in patent litigation relating to wireless communications technology. Plaintiffs in the matter are seeking unspecified damages. The litigation is in early stages and the range of loss is not yet determinable.

Management expects total revenue growth in the low-single-digit range for the next quarter. Services revenue is forecast to continue double-digit growth.
